title: magnolia punduana
intro: Magnolia punduana is a plant species native to India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the Magnoliaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Magnolia punduana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
